Scenario.title: FunnyDream Campaign: Targeted Phishing and Data Exfiltration in Communications Sector
Scenario.overview: Communications Simple A sophisticated spear-phishing campaign targeting communications infrastructure, leveraging internet-facing assets and script-based exploitation to exfiltrate sensitive data.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Script File, Command
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Command and Scripting Interpreter, Command Execution (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2015-2502 (CWE-119): Microsoft Internet Explorer memory corruption vulnerability allowing code execution
stage.description: The attacker sends targeted emails containing malicious hyperlinks to employees, exploiting the vulnerability in internet-facing assets to deliver phishing payloads that compromise endpoint systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2016-20017 (CWE-78): Command injection vulnerability in D-Link DSL-2750B devices
stage.description: Malicious scripts are executed on compromised endpoints, leveraging script file vulnerabilities to deploy a backdoor that establishes persistent access to the network infrastructure.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attacker initiates automated data exfiltration processes, using encrypted command execution mechanisms to steal sensitive communications data from compromised systems.

Scenario.summary: A multi-stage attack beginning with spear-phishing, progressing to script-based exploitation, and culminating in automated data exfiltration targeting communications infrastructure.
